ety: see humāʼe
def: s.m. Name of a fabulous bird commonly regarded in the East as a bird of happy omen (it is supposed to fly constantly in the air and never to touch the ground; and that every head it overshadows will in time wear a crown); — a phœnix; — a large royal eagle; — a bird of paradise (cf. ʻanqā).
more: s. m. 1. An eagle, a phœnix, a bird of paradise. 2. A bird of happy omen, peculiar to the East. (It is supposed that every head it overshadows will in time wear a crown). See عنقا aﻌnḳā.

ety: Pehl. humāi; fr. Zend huma, rt. hu = S. सु (सवति)

more: s. m. 1. See هما humā. 2. Queen Homai.

Where liberal hands reside there Lakshmī goddess ever waits, As to the mason pails on pails of mortar hand his mates. 3. Apnī bībī ko dūr na dūṅgī, rakkhūṅgī nainā hajūrī, Haiṅkal bhī dūṅgī, baddhī bhī dūṅgī, kaṅghī to dūṅgī jarūrī . Song. Sohāg . My daughter I'll not send afar, within my sight shall be her home, A necklace I'll give her, a chain besides, and certainly a comb. 2. The presence chamber, the hall of audience; the court. 3. Your honor; your highness. 4. Government estate. huzūr, yā sadar tahsīl . Collection of revenue by the chief fiscal…

n. f. S. इष् to wish. 1. Wish; desire; will; pleasure. Jo āp kī ichchhā . 2. Solicitation; requisition; demand. 3. Want; need; lack. Phr. The mouth watering; the hand itching. Mahārāj, kyā ichchhā hai? 4. Object; aim; purpose; view; design; intention; intent; scope; drift. Kyā ichchhā hai, jo ghaṛī ghaṛī āte ho? ichchhā bhojan , The longed-for meat; the kind of victuals a man most likes; desiraable food. ich'chhā rakh'nā,—kar'nā , v. n. 1. To desire; wish; long; crave; hanker after. 2. To solicit; ask; request. 3. To aim at; intend; affect; pretend to. Phr. To have at heart; have a mind to; to take one's fancy. Jo ichchhā karo, so pāo .
more: adj. S. अच्छ, Pālī. achchho clear, Opp. of burā . 1. Pleasing; agreeable; pleasant; good. 1. Kyā achchhī āvāz hai! 2. Āchhī āchhī bāteṅ likhīṅ thīṅ chīṭhyā meṅ. Ek rasyā ne bāṅch sunāī hai, Chithyā supne meṅ yār kī āī hai . Rus. Song. Such sweet sweet words were written in that note. An am'rous swain did read it all to me. I dreamed the letter from my lover came. 2. Better; preferable; improved. Kal se achchhā hai . 3. Cheerful; lively; gay ( khush ). 4. Choice; fine; nice ( chokhā .) 1. Achchhā saudā do, to āṅveṅ . 2. Tum baṛe achchhe dost āë! (Ironic.) 5. Unexceptionable; unobjectionable; perfect. 6. Sound; sterling; standard ( kharā ). Achchhā rupyā . 7. Unadulterated; pure. Achchhā dūdh . 8. Natural; simple. 9. True; genuine ( asal ); sea-worthy (a ship). 10. Fresh; in good condition; unimpaired; uninjured; spotless. Achchhā anār . 11. Fair; lovely; attractive; winning; beautiful. Ac…

ety: v.n. fr. عود 'to return,' &c.
def: s.f. A periodical festival, a festival, feast-day, holy-day; the Mohammadan Easter ( = ʻīduʼl-fit̤r); great festivity and rejoicing, festivity, revelry
more: n. f. عود returning. 1. A Mah. religious festival. Hai roz-i-īd, ranjish-i-khātir ko do salām! Ao gale lago mere! kaisī 'nahīṅ nahīṅ?' Āzurdah. This is Īd's festive day, bid anger then adieu! Your arms twine round my neck! what ' no no' how say you? 2. A festival day; festivity; revelry. 1. Āj bhī manā bādā, aë zāhid! Tere nazdīk Īd Īd nahīṅ? Sheftah. What, Saint! wine e'en today eschew, Is Īd no festive day with you? 2. Har roz Īd nest ki halvā khurad kase . P. Prov. Every day is not Īd that Halva one should feast on. (Christmas comes but once a year). īd-ul-fitar, īd , n. f. A Mah. festival observed in the month of Shavvāl , q. v. īd-ul-zuhā ; Pop. baqrīd , n. A Mah. festival observed in the month of Zil hij , q. v. īd ke chānd h. , v. n. To be seen on very rare occasions. Tum to Īd ke chāṅd ho gaë. īd-gāh , n. f. A platform, sometimes screened or enclosed by a brick wall, where Mahome…

s. f. 1. A solemnity, festival, holy day (returning at stated intervals; perhaps, from عود Heb. To return continually to the same act). 2. Easter. عيد الفطر īﻌdu-l-fit̤r or عيد صغير īﻌd-i-ṣag̠ẖīr, A festival held when the fast of Ramaẓān ceases. عيد القربان īﻌdu-l-ḳurbān, A festival held on the tenth of the month ẕu-l-ḥijja, in commemoration of Abrahamʼs offering up Isaac.
subs: or ʻīdu' — ẓ-ẓǒḥā, or ʻīduʼl-qurbān, The festival of victims or sacrifices (held on the tenth of the month ẕuʼl-ḥijja, in commemoration of Abraham's offering up his son Isaac, or Ishmael, according to the Mohammadans)
'The lesser ʻīdʼ = ʻīduʼl-fit̤r, The festival of the breaking of the fast (after Ramazān)

ety: inf. n. iv of دبر 'to follow behind'
def: s.m. Turning back, going back; falling off from allegiance; defection; disloyalty; — decline of good fortune (opp. to igbāl), misfortune, ill-luck, adversity ( = kambaḵẖtī)
more: (inf. iv. of دبر Ch. Was behind, ruled) s. f. Turning back, going back, defection; misfortune, bad luck, adversity, ruin.
subs: (-ko), To be possessed with a spirit of disloyalty or rebellion; to fall away from allegiance; to experience a decline of good fortune; to be overtaken by misfortune ( = kambaḵẖtī ānā).

ety: for A. عدة, inf. n. of عدّ 'to number,' &c.
def: s.f. The time of probation which a divorced woman, or a widow, must wait before she can marry again
more: Mah. n. f. The time of legal probation which a divorced woman or a widow must wait before she marries again, in order to determine whether she is pregnant. iddat meṅ baiṭhnā , v. a. To pass the time of iddat in seclusion from strange men.
subs: v.n. To pass the time of ʻiddat in seclusion.

ety: inf. n. viii of دعا 'to seek, desire'
def: s.m. Claiming (a thing), demanding as a right; claim; pretension.
more: (inf. viii. of دعا) s. m. The act of demanding oneʼs rights, claim, pretension.

def: s.m. Duplication of a letter by tashdīd; coalescence of letters (in Arabic construction).
more: (inf. iv. of دغم) s. m. Reduplication of a letter, or the contraction of one letter into another similar.

ety: rel. n. fr. ʻīd
def: adj. Of ʻīd, relating to ʻīd; — s.f. A present made at ʻīd, or on a festive occasion; an Easter gift; pocket-money given to children at ʻīd; — a kind of verse descriptive of ʻīd, &c.; the paper on which this verse is written; fees paid to a teacher (by pupils) for an ʻīdī written by him.
more: n. f. 1. A present made at or before Id , etc.; an Easter gift. 2. A kind of verse descriptive of Īd , etc. 3. The paper on which the above is written. 4. Fees from Mah. pupils to their teachers in return for the Īdī written by them. 5. Pocket money given to children at Īd .
adj. (used substantively) A present which it is usual to make on the day of īﻌd, an Easter gift.
adj. Relating to īﻌd; a customary present on the day of īﻌd or on some holyday.

def: Edinburgh (the capital city of Scotland)
ex: وقت ختم ہو جانے کی وجہ سے سوال و جواب کا موقع نہ مل سکا۔ نماز کے بعد کھانا کھایا اور پھر ایڈنبرا چل دیئے۔
As time was running out, there was no opportunity for a question and answer session. After prayer, we had food and set off for Edinburgh.

ety: inf. n. iv of دخل 'to enter'
def: s.m. Causing to enter; putting in; insertion; introduction; entry.
more: (inf. iv. of دخل) s. Insertion, introduction.

ety: inf. n. iv of درج 'to move step by step'
def: s.m. The act of inserting one thing in another; folding up or together; inclosing.
more: (inf. iv. of درج) s. m. Act of inserting one thing in another, folding together.

ety: inf. n. iv of درك 'to attain, overtake'
def: s.m. Perception, apprehension, understanding, comprehension.
more: (inf. iv. of درک) s. m. Comprehension, capacity, understanding, genius.
wety: Ultimately from Arabic إِدْرَاك (ʔidrāk).

ety: inf. n. iv of در 'to become copious; to flow'
def: s.m. Lit. 'Causing milk, urine, &c. to flow copiously'; involuntary or copious discharge of urine, diabetes; hard rain; munificence.
more: (inf. iv. of درّ Heb. Flowed freely) s. m. Flowing of milk (from a camel); involuntary discharge of urine, hard rain, flow, munificence, a liberal gift.

def: prop. n. The patriarch Enoch.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اِدْرِیس (idrīs), borrowed from Arabic إِدْرِيس (ʔidrīs).

def: adj. Like this, such, thus. (See aisā.)

ety: inf. n. iv of وفي 'to be full,' &c.
def: s.f. Paying, satisfying, performing a promise, fulfilling an engagement, keeping faith; payment, satisfaction, discharge, &c.
more: (inf. iv. of وفا) s. f. Performing (a promise, &c.), performance, paying, satisfying, fulfilling.
subs: v.t. To pay, discharge, fulfil, make good, &c.

ety: for A. افادة, inf. n. iv of فيد 'to accrue' (advantage)
def: s.m. Imparting advantage, benefiting; tenour, purport; explanation; teaching, instruction; conveying a meaning or signification, denoting.

ety: for A. افاقة, inf. n. iv of فوق 'to sob, breathe hard,' &c.
def: v.n. To recover from sickness, &c.; to be convalescent.
more: n. m. Recovery from sickness, or from a swoon, etc. ( softā ). ifāqā ho'nā , v. n. To be convalescent; to recover from sickness ( ārām honā ).
s. m. (inf. iv. of فوق) Convalescence, recovery.

ety: for A. افاقة, inf. n. iv of فوق 'to sob, breathe hard,' &c.
def: v.n. To recover from sickness, &c.; to be convalescent.
more: s. f. (inf. iv. of فوق) Convalescence, recovery.

ety: for A. عفة, inf. n. of عف 'to abstain,' &c.
def: s.f. Abstinence, continence, chastity, virtue, purity, modesty, decency.
more: n. f. عف abstained. Modesty (عصمت 1). iffat meṅ khalal ḍālnā , G. G., v. a. To outrage modesty.
(from عفّ) s. f. Purity, chastity, continence, virtue, abstinence, modesty.

ety: inf. n. iv of فلس 'to be powerless, needy,' &c.
def: s.m. Poverty, need, want, penury, indigence.
more: (inf. iv. of فلس) s. m. Poverty, penury, indigence, bankruptcy.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic إِفْلاس (ʔiflās, “bankruptcy”).

def: s.f. incorr. for افلاس iflās, q.v.
more: s. f. (dakh.) Poverty, wretchedness ( Harris ): see افلاس iflās.

ety: inf. n. iv of فرد 'to be alone'
def: s.m. Withdrawing from society.
more: (inf. iv. of فرد) s. m. Withdrawing from society.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افراد (afrād), plural form of فرد (fard), from Arabic فَرْد (fard, “individual”).

ety: inf. n. iv of فرط 'to get before'
def: s.f. Plethora
more: (inf. iv. of فرط) s. m. f. Excess, superfluity, abundance. افراط اخلاط ifrāt̤-i-aḵẖlāt̤ or افراط خون ifrāt̤-i-ḵẖūn, Plethora.
subs: v.n. To abound, be plentiful, to be found in abundance.

ety: v.n. fr. عفر 'to roll in the dust,' &c.
def: s.m. A wicked or malignant spirit or jinn, a demon, spectre, ogre, giant; anything frightful or horrible.
more: (from عفر To roll in the dust) s. m. 1. Any thing frightful or horrible. 2. A giant, demon, spectre. (Plur. عفاريت).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عِفْرِیت (ifrīt), from Arabic عِفْرِيت (ʕifrīt).

ety: inf. n iv of فشي 'to spread abroad'
def: s.m. Divulging, disclosing, publishing
more: (inf. iv. of فشي Heb. Was spread abroad) s. m. Divulging, publishing.
subs: s.m. Divulging, or disclosure, of a secret; detection
(-kā), To divulge; to expose, lay bare
To be divulged; to be discovered or detected.

def: v. a. To divulge; expose; lay bare ( khol denā ). ifshā-i-rāz , n. m. Disclosure of a secret; detection. ifshā honā ; H. khulnā , v. n. To be discovered, found out.

ety: inf. n. iv of فطر 'to break a fast'
def: s.m. Breaking a fast; a slight repast with which a fast is broken, a light breakfast
more: (inf. iv. of فطر) s. m. Breaking a fast. افطار کرنا ift̤ār karnā, To break oneʼs fast in the evening after fasting all day, as the Musalmāns do during the month of Ramaẓān.
subs: v.t. To break one's fast in the evening after fasting all day (as the Musalmāns do during the month of Ramaẓān); to take a slight repast by way of breaking a fast.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian افطار (iftār), from Arabic إِفْطَار (ʔifṭār).

def: s.f. Things proper to be eaten in breaking a fast.
more: Mah. n. f. Things proper to be eaten in breaking a fast. Būā, roze kā waqt āyā, iftārī kī taīyāryāṅ karo . Mah. Wom.
wety: From اِفطار (iftār) + ـی (-ī).

ety: inf. n. viii of فخر 'to glory'
def: s.m. Glory, honour, distinction; elegance, gracefulness
more: (inf. viii. of فخر) s. m. Glory, honour, elegance, gracefulness.

ety: inf. n. viii of فري 'to cleave; to cut,' &c.
def: s.m. f. (?) Calumny, false imputation, slander; scandal; fiction.
more: (inf. viii. of فري) s. f. m. Calumny, false imputation, scandal, fiction.
